observed_outcome.main_failure_remaining: low_progress_survival dominates (40/50 trajectories) and no success-like behavior
observed_outcome.main_success_signal: partial_progress exists but reduced to 10 trajectories

failure_repair_outcome.failure_before: low_progress_survival already present (14 in round5) and terminal alignment insufficient; training return was high (+439) but low_progress_survival increased
failure_repair_outcome.repair_attempt: component_recomposition aimed to strengthen near-landing positive reward and terminal alignment, but introduced components (hovering_penalty, leg_bonus, low_alt_stability, terminal_bonus) that over-reward low_progress_survival trajectories
failure_repair_outcome.outcome_after: low_progress_survival became dominant (40/50), partial_progress decreased to 10, training return dropped to 6.26, and many components show poor alignment (hovering_penalty 0.058, leg_bonus 0.103, low_alt_stability 0.269, terminal_bonus 0.047)

lesson: Component recomposition without careful alignment can cause reward to favor low_progress_survival over partial_progress, leading to regression; future mutations must ensure that new components do not inadvertently reward low-quality trajectories and that the overall preference ordering remains correct.
future_guidance: Preserve early_approach and step_progress components as they show high alignment (0.99 each).
Investigate low_progress_survival trajectories to distinguish features from partial_progress and design components that penalize them.
Avoid or redesign components with low preference consistency: hovering_penalty, leg_bonus (current form), low_alt_stability, terminal_bonus may over-reward low-quality.
Strengthen terminal_bonus or terminal_penalty to favor partial_progress over low_progress_survival.
Consider rollback to round5 elite if further regression occurs.
Continue to avoid global survival bonuses without progress gating.

archive_update.reason: Candidate added to archive despite regression because it is still used as parent for next round; training return dropped but no rollback triggered.

uncertainties: No success-like trajectories exist, so success-level preferences cannot be constructed.
Low_progress_survival trajectories are now the majority; their characteristics and how to differentiate from partial_progress are uncertain.
The dramatic drop in training return from +439 to +6 may indicate reward over-inflation in previous elite or mismatch; exact cause of regression (component misalignment vs. scaling) is not isolated.
Component alignment for several new components is low, but the precise contribution of each to the regression is uncertain.
